title: 5 Settlement to Current Accounts
body_text:
5	Settlement to Current Accounts
5.1	Overview of Settlement to Current Accounts
Central 1 uses your current account to settle all financial activity related to the products and services your financial institution uses, including the fees. You can view your Central 1 account information in near-real time in Current Account Services (CAS) Online. Account information is available for each currency type your financial institution maintains with Central 1 (i.e., Canadian dollars, US dollars, Pound Sterling, and Euros).
Account Summaries
Depending on your financial institutionâs preference, CAS Online can be set up with one settlement account (i.e., transactions from multiple branches settle to one account) or with multiple settlement accounts (i.e., transactions settle to different accounts by branch). If your financial institution has accounts in more than one currency, separate sub-accounts are set up in CAS Online and transactions are posted to these sub-accounts appropriately (e.g., CAD or USD). See the Current Account Services (CAS) Online Part for more information on the function of account summaries.
5.2	Central 1 Transaction and Charge Codes
Transaction codes identify the transaction type. The dollar value associated with a transaction code reflects the settlement amount. Charge codes identify the charges associated with a transaction type. The dollar value associated with a charge code reflects the amount of the service charge. See the Transaction and Charge Codes, Form 3667, for a list of these codes for Canadian dollar, US dollar, Pound Sterling, and Euro items.
The transaction codes are reported on the daily reports and the charge codes are reported on the month-end statements:
Account Reconciliation files (PREC and OREC)
Account Activity and Balances Report â CAD (e.g., AIBC/AIOC and DAIL Files)
Account Activity and Balances Report â USD (e.g., AIBU/AIOU and DAIL Files)
Account Activity and Balances Report â EURO (e.g., AIBE/AIOE and DAIL Files)
Account Activity and Balances Report â UK (e.g., AIBK and DAIL Files)
Note:	See the AFT Reports Part for descriptions of the daily statement reports.
The Central 1 monthly statements (e.g., MSC, MSUS, MSUK, MSEU)
These reports and files are available on Central 1âs File and Report Exchange. See the File Exchange and Reports Distribution Part for procedures on how to access these reports.
See the Current Account Services (CAS) Online Part for information on additional reports (e.g., posting advices, account statements, and billing reports).
Related Information
Transaction and Charge Codes,
Form 3667
5.3	Monthly Statements (MSC, MSUS, MSUK, MSEU)
Account information is provided in the monthly statement for each currency type your financial institution maintains with Central 1 (i.e., CAD, USD, Euro, and/or Pound Sterling).
The monthly statement shows transactions along with associated charges and fees. It provides daily activities and totals sorted by your financial institution and the transits associated with your financial institution. Use this report to validate the total monthly volume of transactions on the applicable Central 1 current account. See Figure 1 to Figure 4 for examples of the monthly statements.
Figure 1: Sample Monthly Statement Canadian (MSC)
Figure 2: Sample Monthly Statement US (MSUS)
Figure 3: Sample Monthly Statement UK (MSUK)
Figure 4: Sample Monthly Statement EU (MSEU)
END
